SOLE
MEUNIÈRE

A classic French preparation of delicate sole fish, finished with brown butter
and fresh lemon juice. This simple yet elegant dish highlights the subtle
flavor of the fish without heavy sauces or complicated techniques.

INGREDIENTS:
- 4 whole sole fillets (about 6 oz each)
- 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
- Salt and white pepper to taste
- 4 tbsp butter, divided
- 2 tbsp fresh lemon juice
- Fresh parsley for garnish
- Lemon wedges for serving

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Pat sole fillets dry with paper towels. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Dust fillets lightly with flour, shaking off excess.
3. Heat 2 tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
4. Cook fillets 3-4 minutes per side until golden and opaque.
5. Transfer to a warm plate.
6. Add remaining butter to skillet and cook until it turns golden brown.
7. Drizzle browned butter and lemon juice over fillets.
8. Garnish with fresh parsley and serve immediately with lemon wedges.

SERVING SUGGESTIONS:
Serve with steamed asparagus and new potatoes. A crisp white wine complements well.
